ai_insights.executive_summary: HOGONC's four clinics delivered mixed performance in February 2026, with a network-wide Performance Score averaging 42.75 out of 100 (where 50 represents the company average). Two clinics—NH MO and BCC MO—posted substantial month-over-month gains, associated with improvements in scheduling compliance and chair utilization. However, MTH MO's average patient delay of 13.7 minutes sits 33% above the company benchmark of 10.25 minutes, representing our most actionable opportunity for operational refinement.
highlights: NH MO executed a operational turnaround in February, with Scheduler Compliance climbing from 42.3% in January to 66.7% in February (a 24-point gain), while Chair Utilization rose from 45.2% in January to 69.7% in February. BCC MO similarly strengthened its scheduling discipline, with Scheduler Compliance improving from 47.0% in January to 67.1% in February (a 20-point gain), and iAssign Utilization rising from 76.2% in January to 95.0% in February, now matching the company's highest adoption rate alongside NH MO's 100% utilization.
concerns: We noticed that MTH MO's average delay of 13.7 minutes substantially exceeds the company average of 10.25 minutes, and the clinic also carries the lowest Nurse Utilization in the network at 45.8% compared to the company group average of 53.4%. One area worth exploring together is whether MTH MO's scheduling templates may benefit from review—tighter alignment with iOptimize recommendations (currently 74.9% compliance, above the company average of 69.49%) could further reduce wait time variation. Additionally, PW MO's iAssign Utilization stands at 83.3%, the lowest across HOGONC, compared to company average of 94.7%, suggesting potential gains from targeted adoption support.
recommendations: We recommend MTH MO conduct a focused audit of its appointment spacing and nurse-to-chair assignment workflow. With average delays at 13.7 minutes against the company benchmark of 10.25 minutes, a structured review of how iOptimize scheduling recommendations are being operationalized in real time—and where bottlenecks emerge between chair readiness and nurse availability—could unlock measurable improvement without requiring structural changes.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
